pests.name: Aulacophora Beetle
pests.impact: Eats holes in leaves of young plants, causing significant damage. Larval stage lives in soil and damages roots of young plants.
pests.solution: Destroy old crop residues, start new crops periodically, cover crops with about 200 cm at the start of cultivation, protect natural enemies, physically destroy beetles from the start of damage. If essential, use recommended insecticides: Chlorantraniliprole 185g/1SC (32ml/16L water every 10 days for young plants), Spinosad 25g/1SP (16ml/16L water every 10 days for young plants), or Thiocyclam 50% SP (25g/10L water every 14 days for large vines).

pests.name: Epilachna Beetle
pests.impact: Beetle and larva scrape and eat leaves.
pests.solution: Identify correctly upon seeing damage and collect and destroy beetles, larvae, and pupae. Uproot and destroy old crop residues immediately after harvest. Cover new crop up to about 50 cm high.

pests.name: Fruit Fly
pests.impact: Punctures the skin of young cucurbit fruits and lays 6-12 elongated, creamy white eggs. The legless white maggots that emerge feed on the fruit pulp, causing it to rot. Fruits become irregularly shaped.
pests.solution: Always destroy fruits containing maggots. Uproot and destroy old crops after harvest. Cover new crops up to about 200 cm high. Deep plow soil. Install Cue-lure pheromone traps long-term in the field to destroy male flies. Cover young fruits with polythene or paper. When flowering begins, spray recommended insecticide-mixed protein bait on the underside of leaves at 300-400 cm intervals every 7 days in the morning, or hang as a bait trap.

pests.name: Mite
pests.impact: Scrapes leaf surface and feeds on sap. Sap feeding reduces leaf greenness and makes them brittle.
pests.solution: Destroy old crop residues before new cultivation. Spray water on crops in the evening. Spray recommended miticides.

pests.name: Whitefly
pests.impact: Pierces plant leaves and sucks sap. Vector for viral diseases.
pests.solution: Uproot and destroy old crop residues before starting new cultivation. Cover the crop. Use fertilizers as recommended. Spray water in the evening. Use insecticides as recommended.

pests.name: Aphid
pests.impact: Pierces plant tissues and sucks sap, excreting a sugary, viscous solution. Vectors for viral diseases.
pests.solution: Destroy old crop residues. Cover the crop. Use fertilizers as recommended. Wipe off by hand or with a brush at the early stage of damage. Spray water in the evening. Spray insecticides as recommended. Protect natural enemies like ladybugs, hoverfly larvae, and parasitic wasps.

diseases.name: Powdery Mildew
diseases.impact: First appears as pale yellow spots on mature leaves. Spots enlarge as white powdery mycelium grows on the leaf surface. Severely affected leaves turn brown, shrivel, and defoliate.
diseases.solution: Use recommended fungicides at the early stage of disease development. Remove infected crop residues after harvest.

diseases.name: Downy Mildew
diseases.impact: Angular yellow spots delimited by leaf veins on the upper surface of leaves. Leaves become chlorotic and eventually necrotic, and host plant cells die.
diseases.solution: Use recommended fungicides at the early stage of disease development. Remove infected plant parts after harvest.

diseases.name: Foot Rot / Basal Rot
diseases.impact: Initially, plants show symptoms of temporary wilting. Leaves show yellowing, wilting, and drooping. Eventually, the entire plant dies. Vascular bundles in the basal region turn yellow or brown. White fungal mycelia can be seen at the base of the plant.
diseases.solution: Destroy infected plants along with soil. Improve drainage. Use recommended fungicides at the early stage of disease development.

diseases.name: Gummy Stem Blight
diseases.impact: Typically starts with water-soaked lesions on leaves and fruits. Initially, chlorotic spots may be present, then turn light brown and become irregularly shaped. Dark, cracked, sunken spots appear on fruits. Plant collapse at sunken, bark-furrowed canker sites. Stem lesions often elongate into streaks, and cracks may exude gum. Dark brown to black pycnidia can be seen on fruits, stems, or leaves.
diseases.solution: Deep plow crop residues immediately after harvest to minimize fungal survival. Use recommended fungicides at the early stage of disease development.

diseases.name: Bacterial Wilt
diseases.impact: Sudden wilting of leaves is observed. Eventually, the entire plant wilts. Leaves turn brown and dry. Vascular discoloration (browning). Permanent wilting.
diseases.solution: Destroy infected plants along with soil. Crop rotation with resistant varieties (Okra, Maize). Intercropping with Cruciferae family crops. Avoid moving equipment from infected fields to uninfected fields.

diseases.name: Leaf Curl Virus (Lcv)
diseases.impact: Upward curling of leaves, shortening of internodes, and distortion. Stunting of plants and fruit distortion.
diseases.solution: Control Whitefly vectors by uprooting and destroying old crop residues before starting new cultivation, covering the crop, using fertilizers as recommended, spraying water in the evening, and using insecticides as recommended. Remove infected plants.

diseases.name: Papaya Ringspot Virus (Prsv)
diseases.impact: Mottled spots on leaves. Leaf distortions. Fruit distortion and reduced fruit size. In severe infections, oily spots appear on fruit surface.
diseases.solution: Control Aphid vectors by destroying old crop residues, covering the crop, using fertilizers as recommended, wiping off by hand or with a brush at the early stage of damage, spraying water in the evening, and spraying insecticides as recommended. Protect natural enemies. Remove infected plants.

diseases.name: Cucumber Mosaic Virus (Cmv)
diseases.impact: Green mottling and leaf distortion. Green spots on fruits, turning bright yellow and decaying. Fruit distortion.
diseases.solution: Remove infected plants and use clean seeds.

diseases.name: Zucchini Yellow Mosaic Virus (Zymv)
diseases.impact: Stunting, yellowing, chlorosis (occasional), mottled distorted leaves (blisters). Fruit discoloration and distortion.
diseases.solution: Remove infected plants and control vectors.

diseases.name: Phytoplasma Disease Of Cucurbit Crops
diseases.causal_agent: Transmitted Through Infected Seeds And Leafhoppers
diseases.impact: Small, thickened, and yellowish-green leaves. Shortening of internodes and stunting of plants. Chrysanthemum-like appearance. Greening of flowers.
diseases.solution: Remove infected plants and control leafhopper vectors using a recommended insecticide.
